D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Woad vs Indigo — Same Molecule, Different Plant

European woad (Isatis tinctoria) and Indian indigo (Indigofera tinctoria) both deliver indigotin; the indigo bush yields ~30× more dye per kg, ending Europe's woad monopoly in the 1600s

Indigotin is the chromophore in both. Woad leaves yield ~0.5% indigotin by mass; Indigofera leaves yield 2–5%. When Vasco da Gama opened the Indian sea route in 1498, woad guilds in Toulouse and Erfurt fought to ban "Devil's Dye" — they lost.

Slide to interpolate between woad (left) and indigo (right) yield economics.
